response:
The Silver Lantern Tavern feels like a well-kept secret in the heart of Crescent Heights—dimly lit, warm, and brimming with character. From the moment you step inside, you’re greeted by dark wood paneling, Edison-style bulbs suspended from twisted iron fixtures, and the gentle hiss of a vintage espresso machine in the corner. A faint waft of rosemary and clove greets you as you settle into a plush leather banquette. The ambiance strikes a perfect balance between cozy neighborhood haunt and adventurous destination.

Service at the Silver Lantern is friendly without ever feeling intrusive. Our server, Mariana, knew the menu inside and out—able to recommend a crisp Riesling to pair with our first course and explain the smoky nuance of the tavern’s own barrel-aged stout. Courses arrived at a confident pace, allowing time to savor each bite but never leaving us waiting.

Highlights of the menu include the “Charred Mushroom Polenta,” a creamy cornmeal base dotted with chanterelles and finished with a drizzle of truffle oil. The main event, a slow-braised short rib glazed in tamarind and honey, arrived nestled on a bed of black garlic mashed potatoes—fork-tender and bursting with umami. We also tried the “Miso-Maple Glazed Brussels Sprouts,” which struck a playful balance of sweet, savory, and just the right amount of char. Desserts were no afterthought: the “Smoked Sea Salt Caramel Pudding” was an utterly decadent finale.

If there’s room for improvement, it’s that weekend dining can get quite busy—reservations are essential, and the noise level rises toward a lively hum. Still, the Silver Lantern Tavern offers an unforgettable combination of atmosphere, attentive service, and creative fare.

Rating: 4.5 out of 5 stars
